steps.title: Factor out the greatest common factor
steps.content:
Identify the GCF of all three terms in $2x^3 + 44x^2 + 234x$. Each term contains $2x$:
$$2x^3 + 44x^2 + 234x = 2x(x^2 + 22x + 117)$$
Now find the linear factors in the quadratic expression.

steps.title: Factor the quadratic using factor pairs
steps.content:
To factor $x^2 + 22x + 117$, find two numbers that multiply to 117 and add to 22.

The factor pairs of 117 are: 1 and 117, 3 and 39, 9 and 13.

Check: $9 \times 13 = 117$ and $9 + 13 = 22$ ✓
$$x^2 + 22x + 117 = (x + 9)(x + 13)$$

steps.title: Find the smallest positive value of b
steps.content:
The complete factorization is:
$$2x^3 + 44x^2 + 234x = 2x(x + 9)(x + 13)$$
The linear factors with the form $(x + b)$ are $(x + 9)$ and $(x + 13)$, giving $b = 9$ or $b = 13$. Since we need the smallest positive value of $b$, the answer is 9.
